description: Fix two CI failures: esbuild version mismatch in e2e-test plugin lockfile causing macOS E2E job to fail, and a flaky rapid hot-reload E2E test that fails on slow CI runners.

userStories.title: Update e2e-test plugin lockfile to resolve esbuild version mismatch
userStories.description: Update the e2e-test plugin's package-lock.json so esbuild matches the version installed by the root monorepo, preventing the macOS CI esbuild postinstall validation failure.
acceptanceCriteria: plugins/e2e-test/package-lock.json has esbuild version 0.27.7 (matching root monorepo)
Running `cd plugins/e2e-test && npm ci` succeeds without esbuild version mismatch errors
userStories.notes:
Files:
- plugins/e2e-test/package-lock.json (update esbuild version)

Context:
The `test:e2e` npm script runs `npm ci` in `plugins/e2e-test`, which installs esbuild 0.27.4 from its lockfile. On macOS CI runners, the root monorepo's esbuild binary (0.27.7) is in PATH. When the plugin's esbuild package runs its `install.js` postinstall validation, it finds the 0.27.7 binary but expects 0.27.4, and throws:
```
Error: Expected "0.27.4" but got "0.27.7"
```

The root monorepo has esbuild 0.27.7 (resolved from `^0.27.3` in plugin-tools). The e2e-test plugin's lockfile is stale at 0.27.4.

Changes:
Run `cd plugins/e2e-test && npm install` to update the lockfile. This will resolve esbuild to 0.27.7 (matching `^0.27.3` from the plugin-tools dependency). The `package.json` does not need changes — only the lockfile.

Constraints:
- Do NOT modify plugins/e2e-test/package.json
- Do NOT modify any source code files
- Only the lockfile should change

userStories.title: Fix flaky rapid hot-reload E2E test
userStories.description: Make the 'session ID survives 5 rapid hot reloads' E2E test more resilient on slow CI runners by increasing the spacing between reloads.
acceptanceCriteria: The hot reload loop uses at least 1500ms spacing between reloads (up from 500ms)
The test still verifies that at least 3 hot reloads complete successfully
The test still asserts session ID persistence and tool access after reloads
